properties.remark: VIDEO ON FACEBOOK OF BELOW 1/4 MILE VISIBILITY IN ROCHESTER TX DUE TO BLOWING DUST ON SATURDAY 2/23. NEAREST OBSERVING STATION SHOWED A MAX WIND GUST OF 53 MILES PER HOUR FROM HASKELL WEST TEXAS MESONET STATION.
POWER POLE SNAPPED IN HALF ALONG CR 259 OFF OF HWY 67.
AWOS STATION SWW, SWEETWATER.
ELECTRIC LINES DOWN THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY. PORTABLE BUILDINGS BLOWN OVER ALONG I-20.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED AN 18 WHEELER BLOWN OVER AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 84 AND INTERSTATE 20.
REPORTED A WIND GUST BETWEEN 60 AND 70 MPH.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 4 SSW BALLINGER. POWER POLE SNAPPED IN HALF ALONG CR 259 OFF OF HWY 67.
ROOF COMPLETELY PEELED OFF OF THE CALVARY BAPTIST CHURCH ON ARMSTRONG STREET.
ROOF BLOWN OFF A BARN. PHONE LINES DOWN ALONG HWY 180.

THE TORNADO RIPPED OFF SEVERAL ROOFS, BLEW OUT WINDOWS AND DAMAGED TREES AND POWER LINES. IT TURNED A ONE BY FOUR PIECE OF WOOD INTO A MISSILE. THIS MISSILE WENT THROUGH A METAL EAVE OF A RESIDENCE AND INTO THE EXTERIOR WALL OF THE RESIDENCE JUST ABOVE THE WINDOW. THE DAMAGING WINDS ALSO TURNED THE LID OF A DEER FEEDER INTO A MISSILE AND LODGED IT INTO THE SIDE OF A BUSINESS. THE DAMAGING WINDS BLEW OUT AN OVERHEAD DOOR ON THE FIRE STATION.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED POWER POLES DOWN, ROOF DAMAGE, AND WINDOWS BLOWN IN THROUGHOUT THE CITY OF JUNCTION.

ALONG ITS PATH, THE TORNADO OVERTURNED A SMALL RESIDENCE AND THREW IT FOR 50 FEET, THANKFULLY THE RESIDENT OF THE HOME WAS UNINJURED. THE TORNADO TORE OFF SEVERAL ROOFS. IT DESTROYED AWNINGS AND POWER LINES, SNAPPED POWER POLES AND TREES, AND DAMAGED A GRANDSTAND. THE TWISTER WRAPPED THE METAL OF A ROOF TIGHTLY AROUND A TREE AS IT BLEW OUT A WALL IN A BUILDING.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN ZEPHYR AND SOME POSSIBLE DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS.
QUARTER TO HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 153 AND HIGHWAY 395 BY A SKYWARN SPOTTER.
PICTURE OF QUARTER TO HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL RECEIVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

PERSON AT TWISTFLOWER RANCH OFF OF COUNTY ROAD 208 SAID THAT HAIL BROKE HIS WINDSHIELD. HE DID NOT HAVE AN ESTIMATE AS TO THE SIZE OF A HAIL AT THE TIME BUT WHEN HE WENT OUTSIDE AFTER SUNRISE THIS MORNING, HE SAW GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL ON THE GROUND.
MEASURED BY N5FTL STERLING CITY MESONET.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM ROCHELLE. SOCIAL MEDIA HAD A PICTURE OF THE QUARTER SIZE HAIL.
SOCIAL MEDIA HAD A PICTURE OF THE QUARTER SIZE HAIL.

THERE WAS LOTS OF PEA SIZE HAIL. ALSO, WIND GUSTS OF 60 MPH IN EARLY.

THE SCHLEICHER COUNTY SHERIFF OFFICE REPORTED A ROOF BLOWN OFF OF A RESIDENCE 12 MILES NORTH OF ELDORADO, ON COUNTY ROAD 431.

THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE TO A RESIDENCE 12 MILES NORTH OF ELDORADO, IN NORTHERN SCHLEICHER COUNTY. LARGE LIVE OAK TREES WERE BLOWN OVER, A WINDOW WAS BLOWN OUT OF A HOUSE, AND A GARAGE DOOR WAS DAMAGED.

TREES WERE UPROOTED, HOME AND ROOF WERE DAMAGED, AND A WATER TROUGH FROM AN UNKNOWN LOCATION WAS BLOWN INTO THE YARD.

DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO A COUPLE OF HOMES, A HIGH SCHOOL, AND A WATER TOWER ON THE NORTH SIDE OF BALLINGER. CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO A BASEBALL STADIUM IN THAT AREA.
